5_Black Tongue.story: My first Jordans.  They were a birthday gift from my parents in November 1990.

16_Volt.story: I really loved these shoes when i first saw them in 1989.  Back then we called them Bo Jackson's.  The original colorway was Citron, but I prefer this colorway.  I'm hoping to add the White Infrared version to my collection at some point.

4_Black Cement.story: Although the Fire Red 4s were my first love, these are a close second.  I was watching game five of the Bulls v Cavs with my dad when Michael Jordan hit "the shot".  I paid way over retail to add these shoes to my collection.

4_Fire Red.story: I first saw this shoe in 1989 in my local shoe store and immediately fell in love.  A few people in my class had these, and I remember being quite envious of them.  This was the first sneaker I bought when I started this collection.

10_Steel.story: I grabbed these for the 1994 basketball season. These were the first shoes that I wore exclusively for basketball.  I would carry them around all day in my backpack and take them out for practice/games.

11_Flu Game.story: I had the original release of this sneaker, but in the Playoff colorway.

15_Hot Lava.story: The first pair of Nike sneakers with an air bubble that I owned. My parents bought these for me as a graduation gift from elementary school in June 1990.  I wore them pretty much every day from June until November when they were replaced with the Black Tongue Air Jordan 5.

21_Purple.story: My father and I went to about five different stores looking for these in June 1993.  Glad to finally add these to my collection.

8_Playoffs.story: In 1993, I went with my father to look for the Air Force Max CB.  After hitting about five stores, I "settled" for these.

9_Olive.story: My first retro Jordan was this silhouette in French Blue.  I bought them in May 2002 and debuted them at Attack of the Clones in Farmingdale New York.
